title: Mohria burniifolia
taxonomy: Mohria burniifolia is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It belongs to the genus Mohria, which comprises several species of ferns. The classification of Mohria burniifolia places it within the phylum Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The family Blechnaceae is a part of the order Polypodiales, which includes many species of ferns. Mohria burniifolia's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species.

intro: Mohria burniifolia, also known as Mohria, is a type of fern plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, Mohria has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mohria include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mohria is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds.
